Notes: Top-level initial transition goes to Active.
Main behavior is nested in Active.
Active initial state enters ReadyForNextItem.
ReadyForNextItem represents the ready-to-scan state.
Invalid barcode loops in ReadyForNextItem via scanBarcode with missing code.
The invalid barcode guard is modeled as !codeExists.
Invalid barcode performs showError.
Invalid product code returns to ReadyForNextItem after the entry step.
The missing product code condition is modeled as !codeExists.
Invalid product code performs showError.
Valid product code leads to WeighingItem through an entry-code state.
The valid product code condition is modeled as codeExists.
Valid barcode leads to the security weight check after the item is placed.
The valid barcode condition is modeled as codeExists.
The scan action only shows the item and does not add it to the bill.
Cancel everything from ReadyForNextItem goes to WaitingForOverride.
The clear-all override type is implied by later guards but not explicitly set on the transition.
removeItem from ReadyForNextItem goes to WaitingForOverride.
The removeItem transition requires billHasItems.
The item override type is implied by later guards but not explicitly set.
addBag from ReadyForNextItem enters WeighingBag.
Pay from ReadyForNextItem enters WaitingForPayment when items exist.
The pay transition is guarded by billHasItems.
The pay transition sends the payment request.
WeighingItem represents weighing an item without a barcode.
cancelWeighing from WeighingItem returns to ReadyForNextItem.
After itemWeighed the model proceeds toward the security weight check.
The weighed item is shown but no action adds it to the bill.
SecurityWeightCheck represents the security scale check.
A matching security weight returns to ReadyForNextItem.
The matching condition is represented by securityWeightMatches.
A security weight mismatch enters WaitingForOverride.
The mismatch condition is represented by securityWeightMismatch.
The item override type is not explicitly set although item clearing is later possible.
cancelEverything from SecurityWeightCheck enters WaitingForOverride.
The clear-all type is only implicit in the cancelEverything path.
WeighingBag is present for customer bags.
After a bag is weighed the model returns to ReadyForNextItem.
bagWeighed increments and displays the bag count.
cancelWeighing from WeighingBag returns to ReadyForNextItem.
WaitingForOverride represents waiting for staff override.
All modeled entries to override turn on the staff light.
Successful exits from override turn off the light.
Correct item override returns to ReadyForNextItem.
The guard uses codeCorrect and itemOverride.
The item override transition clears the item.
Correct clear-all override returns to ReadyForNextItem.
The guard uses codeCorrect and clearAllOverride.
The clear-all override transition clears everything.
Incorrect override code loops in WaitingForOverride.
The incorrect code guard is modeled as !codeCorrect.
Timeout warning is only modeled from ReadyForNextItem rather than all non-payment non-override active states.
Incorrect override code shows an error.
A history state H is included for continuing after the countdown.
TimeoutWarning represents the countdown state.
The timeout warning transition starts the countdown.
Countdown timeout returns to the active ready state.
Countdown timeout clears everything.
Returning to ReadyForNextItem implies readiness but no explicit next-customer reset action is modeled.
WaitingForPayment represents the payment-requested state.
Payment failure returns to ReadyForNextItem.
Payment failure shows an error.
Payment cancellation returns to ReadyForNextItem.
Payment success returns to ReadyForNextItem.
Payment success prints the bill.
The model returns to ready after success but does not explicitly reset for a new customer.
Extra intermediate states for item display and code entry are reasonable refinements.
Extra transitions such as placing items and pay-without-items do not fundamentally conflict.
No fundamentally incorrect extra guards are present.
Extra display and light actions are harmless or consistent.
No incorrect extra composite states are present.
No extra regions are modeled.
No incorrect extra history state is present.
